title: Crumbly Brazilian Black Bean Stew
description: A hearty, umami-packed onepot black bean stew with sauteed vegetables and a crisp, crumbly topping, perfect for a sugarfree diet.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic; sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the sliced bell pepper, julienned carrot, and diced zucchini, cooking until just tender, ~3 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, smoked paprika, bay leaf, salt, and pepper; cook for 2 minutes to release flavors.
Pour in the cooked black beans and 1 cup of vegetable broth (or water). Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 10 minutes to meld the flavors.
Add the fresh spinach and let it wilt, about 2 minutes. Remove the bay leaf.
Transfer the stew to a shallow baking dish, spreading evenly.
Mix the toasted cassava flour with the crushed roasted chickpeas; sprinkle liberally over the stew to form a crumbly crust.
Bake in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven for 15 minutes, or until the topping is golden and crispy.
Remove from the oven, let rest 5 minutes, then serve hot, optionally garnished with chopped cilantro.
